body.chiefComplaint: A 35-year-old woman presented with a one-year history of mobile teeth in the lower left molar region and progressive difficulty chewing associated with pain and reduced mouth opening.
A 17-year-old Asian woman from a rural, animal‐husbandry family presented with a 1‐month history of abdominal pain, nocturnal fevers, and worsening lower back pain.
A 53-year-old man with no significant medical history presented with a headache.
An 8-month-old child presented with progressive head enlargement since birth, global developmental delay (developmental age equivalent to 3 months), and cranial bruits.
A previously healthy woman was admitted with a 20-day history of recurrent headache and fever and a 3-day history of dyspnea.
A 55-year-old woman presented with a 6-day history of diffuse abdominal pain radiating to the flanks, nausea, and chronic constipation.
A 74-year-old woman presented with a 2-day history of dyspnea and bilateral chest pain, continuous and worse on exertion; she denied cough, hemoptysis, fever, night sweats, or weight loss.
An 81-year-old man presented to the emergency department with presyncope.

A 60-year-old postmenopausal woman with a history of total abdominal hysterectomy 20 years earlier for fibroids presented with a 1-year history of clitoral enlargement, hirsutism, deepening of voice, decreased libido, and increasing abdominal girth.
A 22-year-old athletic man with no other medical history presented with two episodes of acute tetraparesis.

A 73-year-old man with ischemic heart disease and a bioprosthetic aortic valve implanted in 2009, type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, hypercholesterolemia, stage 4 chronic kidney disease, and peptic ulcer disease presented with a 2-week history o
A 37-year-old woman presented with a 1-week history of pain in the toes of her left foot.
A 17-year-old Caucasian woman presented with acute right lower abdominal pain.

A 63-year-old woman with metastatic myoepithelial carcinoma of the left parotid gland, type 2 diabetes (managed with metformin until recently), hypertension, atrial fibrillation, and hypothyroidism presented for routine chemotherapy infusion and was
A 16-year-old Iranian girl presented with sudden-onset bilateral horizontal gaze palsy that developed overnight.
A 33-year-old woman was found unresponsive and diaphoretic by paramedics.
A term female newborn (3.700 kg) was born vaginally without prenatal complications.
A 41-day-old female infant was referred for evaluation of recurrent electrolyte disturbances.
An 8-year-old girl was brought to the emergency department with reduced consciousness and an 8-day history of high fever, accompanied by vomiting, epigastric pain, and a mild cough.
A 48-year-old man of African descent was brought to the emergency department unresponsive after a generalized tonic–clonic seizure.
A 35‐year‐old man with a 5‐year history of recurrent palpitations and multiple emergency department visits.
A 65-year-old man was brought to the emergency department because of progressive fatigue, diffuse myalgia, and altered mental status.
A 25-year-old Asian woman presented with a 3-week history of constant bilateral heel pain and difficulty walking.
A 22-year-old Chinese woman was referred for evaluation of oligomenorrhea.
A 54-year-old man presented with right flank pain of eight months’ duration.
A 4-week-old male infant, born at 30 weeks’ gestation (birth weight 1665 g; Apgar scores 8 and 9), was admitted at birth for respiratory distress and cyanosis requiring intubation.
A 39-year-old Japanese man presented with three weeks of left chest pain and a rash on his hands and feet.
A 43-year-old woman presented with a 2-week history of progressive dyspnea.

A 25-year-old Caucasian woman with a history of moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue, treated 16 months earlier with hemi-glossectomy and chemoradiation, presented with a 1-month history of progressively worsening severe, s

A 66-year-old man with sleep apnoea and a history of junctional tachycardia ablation presented with acute dyspnoea shortly after undergoing urgent percutaneous coronary intervention for an inferior ST-elevation myocardial infarction.
A 69-year-old woman reported a 5-year history of recurrent febrile episodes every 6–8 weeks.
A 30-year-old man first noted right calf muscle cramps in 2013.
A male infant was born at 39 weeks’ gestation via non-elective cesarean delivery for failed induction of labor in the setting of intrauterine growth restriction.
A 61-year-old homeless African American man with hypertension presented with 1 week of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain.
A 49-year-old woman with type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, and hypertension presented with several weeks of cramping abdominal pain, vomiting, and frequent watery, nonbloody diarrhea.

A 37 year-old man with HIV infection and a recent history of cryptococcal meningitis, treated with amphotericin followed by fluconazole, began ART (zidovudine, lamivudine, efavirenz) 21 days after meningitis treatment completion.

A 40‐year‐old woman with asthma, hypertension, and a 20‐year history of psoriatic arthritis involving both temporomandibular joints presented with a 4‐month history of a hard, tender swelling (1.5×2.0 cm) in the left preauricular region.

A 36-year-old woman with no prior liver disease presented in August 2008 with a 5-day history of constant, sharp right upper quadrant abdominal pain associated with nausea and fatigue but no vomiting.
A 23-year-old man from Cameroon presented with acute-onset, right-sided scrotal pain and swelling while in the United States for military training.
A 65‐year‐old woman presented with two months of persistent left upper‐quadrant abdominal pain and multiple episodes of nonbilious, nonbloody vomiting of recently ingested food.

A 69-year-old woman with metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (peritoneal and hepatic metastases) enrolled in a trial of SBP-101 combined with nab-paclitaxel and gemcitabine presented with a 6-month history of progressive blurred vision and vi
A 64-year-old woman was found unresponsive by her husband in their non–air-conditioned home during a heatwave, with outdoor temperatures above 33°C (90°F).
A 62‐year‐old woman presented with angular stomatitis and intermittent mild-to-moderate abdominal pain for 6 months.

A 65-year-old woman with metastatic malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor on a clinical trial agent and no known germline mutations presented with 6 weeks of nausea, progressive gait imbalance, and falls.
A 49-year-old man presented with a 6-month history of headache.
A 51-year-old Japanese man presented with a 2-month history of painless right vision loss.

cases.expected: Congenital insensitivity to pain and anhydrosis

body.chiefComplaint: A 19-year-old male, born in 1990 to consanguineous parents, was referred for evaluation of multisystem involvement.
A 17-year-old male presented with several hours of right lower quadrant abdominal pain associated with nausea and subjective fevers.

A 73-year-old woman with a history of poorly differentiated caecal adenocarcinoma (T4N2M0) treated by right hemicolectomy, incomplete adjuvant chemotherapy, and radiotherapy to para-aortic lymph nodes, presented three years after radiotherapy with a
A 13‐year‐old boy was referred to pediatric dentistry for irregularly placed upper and lower front teeth and unerupted teeth.
A 47-year-old woman was admitted with a 10-day history of remittent fevers up to 39°C, fatigue, and sore throat.
A 16-year-old girl presented with a 4-day history of increasing difficulty swallowing solid foods and a painless globus sensation.
A 32-year-old man presented with excessive tearing of the left eye and mild ocular pain.
A 43-year-old man with no prior lung disease presented to the emergency department with sudden-onset left-sided chest pain and progressive shortness of breath.
A 16-year-old boy noted a progressively enlarging lump at his posterior left knee over one year.
An 84-year-old woman was admitted with a one-day history of left lower abdominal pain and nausea without fever.
A 76-year-old man presented with a 5-year history of progressive leg weakness.
A 13-year-old Sudanese girl presented with a 2-day history of colicky abdominal pain, absolute constipation, and vomiting of all ingested food.
A 72-year-old woman was referred for progressive symmetrical proximal muscle weakness.
A 47-year-old woman with HIV-1 infection presented with a 10-day history of diffuse maculopapular rash, fever, myalgias, arthralgias, choluria, pruritus, and right upper-quadrant abdominal pain.
A 58-year-old woman presented with a history since February 2008 of painful left axillary lymphadenopathy accompanied by low-grade fever.
A 52-year-old man presented with progressive right upper limb weakness and sensory disturbance.
A previously healthy 5‐year‐old girl presented with 9 hours of intermittent, moderate–severity epigastric pain radiating to the right lower quadrant.
A 29-year-old woman presented with lower abdominal pain.
A 3-year-old girl presented with gradual-onset abdominal pain and non-bilious vomiting over 2 days.
A 33 4/7-week, 2.22-kg female neonate was born after spontaneous vaginal delivery following premature rupture of membranes.

A 71-year-old man with coronary artery disease, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, gout, and benign prostatic hyperplasia was transferred from a long-term acute care facility with a 2-day history of right wrist and hand pain, tenderness, and swelling.

A 30-year-old man with psoriatic arthritis (on adalimumab for 6 years), hereditary spherocytosis, and a history of cutaneous herpes zoster infection presented with two weeks of intermittent fevers and headaches and a 10-lb weight loss over 9 months.
A previously healthy 9-month-old boy was transferred after 8 days of fever and new-onset macroscopic hematuria.
A 69-year-old Hispanic woman was referred for progressively worsening blurry vision, right eye greater than left.
An 18-year-old woman presented with a 1-year history of slowly enlarging gingival overgrowth in the left posterior mandible that interfered with chewing but was painless.

cases.expected: Congenital anterolateral tibial bowing and polydactyly

body.chiefComplaint: A 15-month-old boy was referred for evaluation of a right lower limb deformity.
A 67‐year‐old man with type 2 diabetes mellitus and nephropathy presented with a 4‐month history of an asymptomatic rash and swelling over his right thigh.
A 6-year-old girl was admitted with a 5-day history of fever (to 39.5 °C) and a 2-week history of unilateral clear tearing from the right eye.

An 82-year-old man with chronic atrial fibrillation and type 2 diabetes mellitus presented with a 3-month history of worsening peripheral edema and pleural effusion despite escalating diuretic therapy.

A 70-year-old woman with atrial fibrillation on enoxaparin, liver cirrhosis, type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and a history of venous thrombus presented with a 5-day history of left arm weakness, numbness in the left fourth and fifth digits an
A 37-year-old man presented with a 3-month history of progressive skin thickening, initially on his torso and then spreading diffusely, accompanied by a 20–30-lb weight loss and fatigue.
A 72-year-old Caucasian woman with Sézary syndrome, previously treated with interferon alfa-2b and photopheresis, began romidepsin therapy (14 mg/m2 on days 1, 8, and 15) with bexarotene.
A 51-year-old woman with Crohn’s disease on infliximab presented with a 2-day history of a bullous rash on her left arm, axilla, and lateral chest wall accompanied by subjective fever.
A 4-year-old boy was evaluated for a 1-year history of a slowly enlarging mass in the right cheek.

A 34-year-old Japanese man with a history of gastric ulcer treated with esomeprazole presented with 2 days of fever to 40 °C, headache, myalgia, fatigue, and vomiting starting 2 days after a finger laceration during sewage-plumbing work.

A 52‐year‐old man with Addison’s disease on lifelong corticosteroid replacement and a history of lateral epicondylitis presented with a 7‐day history of severe redness around his right elbow accompanied by intense burning and stinging.
A 26-year-old man with no history of hypertension or heart disease presented with a 3-day history of upper abdominal pain.
A 26-year-old man was admitted for evaluation of recurrent hepatitis and persistent cytopenias.

A 70-year-old Caucasian man with IgG-kappa multiple myeloma diagnosed one year ago (initial K-FLC 685 mg/dL, λ 0.13 mg/dL; no lytic bone lesions or organ involvement) presented with 3 months of worsening dyspnea, dysphagia, and weight loss.
A 34-year-old woman presented with a 6-year history of a painless swelling of the lower jaw, initially 1 cm in diameter on the left, which gradually enlarged to involve both sides of the face.
A 68-year-old man with high-grade lymphoma diagnosed four months earlier presented with confusion.
A 28-year-old man presented with a tonic–clonic seizure, left arm paresis, paraparesis, two months of gait disturbance, and fever to 38 °C.
A 35-year-old African American man presented with a one-month history of worsening low back and bilateral leg pain, intermittent night sweats, and a 32-kg unintentional weight loss over one year.
A 60-year-old man presented with a 1-month history of right lower back and abdominal pain.
A 41-year-old woman with no prior medical history was referred for evaluation of suspected early breast cancer.
A 50-year-old non-smoker Caucasian woman with no past medical history presented with chronic mild right upper quadrant abdominal pain for one year.

A 58-year-old woman was referred for evaluation of 8 months of progressive shortness of breath, intermittent fever and chills, productive (non–blood-stained) cough, and a 25-kg unintentional weight loss over 6 months.
A 37-year-old previously healthy man presented with four days of progressive weakness of all four limbs, dysphagia, drooling, nausea, and vomiting.
A 55-year-old man presented with 1 month of edema and 2 weeks of decreased urine output, fever with chills, breathlessness, anorexia, nausea, and recurrent vomiting.
A 63-year-old woman presented with a 1-year history of atypical right upper-quadrant abdominal pain, significant fatigue, and weight loss.
A 22-month-old Caucasian girl presented with low-grade fever, cough, decreased activity, poor oral intake, and bilateral jaw-line swelling.
A 79-year-old white man presented with sudden onset of right-sided ophthalmoplegia, diplopia, and ptosis accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and headache.
A 46-year-old woman was referred for evaluation of new-onset heart failure.
A 10-year-old boy of West African origin, born to non-consanguineous parents, presented with a one-year history of bilateral knee pain radiating into his legs.
